subject: Biology
title: Mycorrhizal Networks
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Mycorrhizal Networks.

hook.text: Beneath a forest floor, fungi and tree roots form a vast network scientists call the 'Wood Wide Web.'

buildup.text: Mycorrhizal fungi wrap around roots and trade minerals for sugars the tree produces.

discovery.text: Trees share nutrients through this fungal network—mother trees send sugar to struggling seedlings.

twist.text: Some plants cheat—they tap the network for resources without giving anything back.

climax.text: About 90% of plant species depend on mycorrhizal partnerships to survive.

punchline.text: Forests are connected underground by fungi.

quiz.question: What do mycorrhizal fungi trade with tree roots?
